text: Hello everyone and thank you for joining us today.
I'm Sara Kouda, the MENA Regional Director with the Committee to Protect Journalists
and I'm honored to be joined with this group of journalists and media leaders from Sudan, Yemen and Gaza.
Today we turn our attention to journalists who are covering the wars and conflicts in Gaza, Sudan and Yemen
where reporting becomes more dangerous and more essential.
I'm honored to be joined by Waal al-Dahdouh from Gaza.
He is the Chief of Al Jazeera in Gaza.
Waal al-Dahdouh's decade of reporting and personal resilience in the face of profound loss
has made him one of the most important voices documenting the genocide in Gaza.
From Sudan, we are joined by Raghdan.
Raghdan Orsoud is the co-founder of Beam Reports whose work is examining how disinformation, technology and conflict intersect to shape narratives and accountability.
And from Yemen, we welcome Mustafa Nasir, Chairman of the Studies and Economic Media Center,
a leading voice on governance and the challenges facing journalists working in prolonged conflicts.
Jala is a Palestinian journalist coming from the West Bank
and she kindly offered to provide translation to this session.
Palestinian journalists in Gaza carry out their work and duty while living through a genocide.
You personally continued your coverage after suffering a profound loss.
How do you keep carrying your job and doing your work despite the impact of the war on you?
And this question is one of the hardest and the most painful ones.
especially for the journalists who lived inside Gazette during the war.
